params.text: 81-year-old female with active diagnoses of hypertension (I10), long-term medication use (Z79.899), and heart failure (I50.9). Vital signs stable: BP 141/71, HR 86, SpO2 96%. Labs notable for creatinine 1.1 and normal troponin. Patient presents with multiple readmission risk factors including advanced age, active heart failure, and polypharmacy. Readmission risk assessed as elevated. Recommend comprehensive discharge planning with early outpatient follow-up to mitigate readmission risk.

details.reason: Heart failure management optimization prior to discharge
Heart failure management optimization prior to discharge

params.message: 81-year-old female with heart failure, hypertension, and polypharmacy. Readmission risk elevated due to age and comorbidities. Cardiology consult ordered for HF optimization. Please coordinate timely outpatient cardiology and primary care follow-up within 7 days post-discharge. Ensure medication reconciliation completed and patient education provided on heart failure symptom monitoring.

data.message: 81-year-old female with heart failure, hypertension, and polypharmacy. Readmission risk elevated due to age and comorbidities. Cardiology consult ordered for HF optimization. Please coordinate timely outpatient cardiology and primary care follow-up within 7 days post-discharge. Ensure medication reconciliation completed and patient education provided on heart failure symptom monitoring.

params.plan_text: Discharge to home with services. Cardiology follow-up within 7 days for heart failure optimization. Primary care follow-up within 3-5 days for medication reconciliation and blood pressure monitoring. Home health nursing for medication management and heart failure education. Daily weights; call provider for weight gain >3 lbs in 1 day or >5 lbs in 1 week, worsening shortness of breath, or swelling. Low sodium diet. Prescriptions: continue home medications with adjustments per cardiology recommendations.
